entities.name: RURAL DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: A political topic and process aimed at improving the quality of life and economic well-being of people living in rural areas.

entities.name: ASPIRATIONS
entities.description: The goals, desires, and hopes of individuals or communities, which are complex and heterogeneous.

entities.name: RURAL POPULATION
entities.description: The people residing in rural areas whose needs and demands are central to development debates.

entities.name: URBAN INCOME EARNING OPPORTUNITIES
entities.description: Economic prospects available in cities that attract rural residents.

entities.name: RURAL FARMING COMMUNITIES
entities.description: Groups of people engaged in agriculture within rural settings.

entities.name: NATIONAL FOOD PROVISION
entities.description: The supply of food for a country's population, primarily supported by rural farming.

entities.name: KENYA
entities.description: The East African country where the research on regional aspirations was conducted.

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL AND OFF-FARM POTENTIAL
entities.description: The capacity of a region to support both farming and non-farming economic activities.

entities.name: DEVELOPMENT INITIATIVES
entities.description: Planned programs or projects intended to foster rural development.

entities.name: HOUSEHOLD SURVEY DATA
entities.description: A quantitative data collection method used to gather information from households.

relationships.description: Aspirations of the rural population should inform and shape rural development strategies
The rural population possesses diverse and heterogeneous aspirations
Rural farming communities are essential for ensuring national food provision
The study examines the aspirations of the rural population within three regions of Kenya
The potential for agricultural and off-farm activities acts as a framing influence on aspirations
Development initiatives should be redesigned to align more closely with local aspirations
Household survey data can be used to enrich the understanding of complex aspirations
Urban income opportunities are often polarized against rural farming in rural development debates
